# UC-17.1.74 · ISE iPSK / MAB / 802.1X Mode Distribution by SSID and Mixed-Mode Anomaly Detection

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Different wifi networks should use the right kind of login. Corporate wifi should require strong proof; guest wifi should be simpler. We watch for mixed setups that weaken security on important networks.*

---

## Description

Tracks the distribution of authentication modes per SSID. Detects unexpected mixed-mode usage — e.g., a corporate SSID accepting both 802.1X and MAB (MAB is weaker; should not coexist on a strong-auth SSID), or a guest SSID seeing certificate-based auth (suggests misconfigured supplicant).

## Value

Mixed-mode SSID config is a common security regression — typically the result of operations staff adding MAB exceptions to handle problem devices. Tracking distribution exposes drift and lets the security team enforce policy.

## Implementation

Schedule every 1h.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Add a MAB authorization rule to a corporate 802.1X SSID. UC-17.1.74 detects mixed mode.

### Negative scenario

All-802.1X SSID. No fire.

## Detailed Implementation

Standard pattern.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=5200
| stats count by ssid, AuthenticationMethod
| eventstats sum(count) as total by ssid
| eval pct=round(count/total*100, 2)
| where pct < 100 AND pct > 0
```

## Visualization

Per-SSID auth-mode pie; per-SSID mode trend over 30d.

## Known False Positives

- Intentional dual-mode SSIDs (legacy device support) — exclude in `ise_dualmode_ssid_exceptions.csv`.
- Test/lab SSIDs.

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 — Wireless Authentication](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/security/ise/3-4/admin_guide/b_ise_admin_3_4/m_secure_wireless_access.html)
